scope.question: Can one field support retrieval, independent cost/risk selection, and evolving revision under a later equal-budget experiment?

forbidden_gold_sources: candidate rank emitted by an evaluated arm

independent_selection_action.required_separation_case: The unique relevance argmax is unsafe, constraint-invalid, or cost-suboptimal under the same evidence state.

label_authority: KEEP, SUPERSEDE, CONTRADICT, and COMPENSATE source labels
fact, revision, event, evidence, and source identities
registered branch confluence expectation or non-confluence reason

required_oracles: current cut for every registered query time
as-of cut for every registered query time

independent_selection_action: generated TextWorld or ALFWorld development worlds

multihop_cross_document: fresh PhantomWiki worlds generated after seed commitment
frozen unseen public split with contamination audit
independent_selection_action: held-out generated worlds and seeds after commitment
evolving_knowledge: frozen EvolvingQA, StreamingQA, or SituatedQA blocks
unreleased synthetic revision blocks generated after commitment

rules: Public benchmark labels are never described as technically sealed.
Every frozen split, source block, query set, candidate set, evaluator, and oracle is commit- and byte-hash-bound before measurement.
Contamination audit records prior exposure, generation seed commitment, source license, and excluded overlap.
Only server-generated fresh worlds or unreleased blocks created after the G4 commitment may be called sealed.
Development labels and confirmatory labels cannot share examples, generated worlds, seeds, or revision blocks.

arm_interface_expectations.mutable_state: Exactly one B-scalar field and one registered update rule across retrieve, select, and revise.
must: Read one versioned field cut and provenance through three registered typed ports.
Use byte-identical task adapters, candidate transforms, and decoders to role B.
Expose parameter and serialized-state inventories to the G3 verifier.
must_not: Use task ID or head ID as a feature.
Hide task lookup tables, constants, routers, optimizers, or mutable state outside the B-scalar inventory.

arm_interface_expectations.mutable_state: Registered retrieve, select, and revise heads whose total mutable scalar count is exactly B.
must: Share byte-identical graph, embeddings, candidates, runtime, training labels, task adapters, transforms, and decoders with role A.
Select the registered head only through the typed port.
Expose per-head and total parameter and serialized-state inventories to the G3 verifier.
must_not: Use a hidden shared router or additional optimizer state.
Use 3B capacity as the confirmatory comparator; a 3B variant is diagnostic only.

arm_interface_expectations.purpose: Calibration control for revision correctness without learned shared-field attribution
arm_interface_expectations.mutable_state: No learned revision scalar; deterministic version and temporal filter state only.
must: Consume the exact same compiled revision events, query times, evidence, and source bindings as roles A and B.
Return current and as-of answers by hard revision ID, valid_time, observed_at, and operation semantics.
Preserve contradiction and compensation history and reject tampered or ambiguous cuts.
must_not: Choose revision labels using similarity or retrieval rank.
Compete as evidence that a learned shared field is effective.

arm_interface_expectations.purpose: Load-bearing negative control for temporal/versioning tests
arm_interface_expectations.mutable_state: No versioned revision state; intentionally collapses revisions to an unversioned latest-value interface.
must: Use the same input/output envelope and evaluator path as roles A, B, and C.
Be evaluated on stale, current, as-of, repeated-revision, contradiction, and compensation cases.
Remain labelled as a negative control regardless of observed score.
must_not: Receive valid_time, observed_at, revision ID, or supersession metadata through a hidden side channel.
Serve as the primary confirmatory winner or support a public efficacy claim.

gates.requirement: All three task gold labels derive from frozen task outcomes/evidence/operations and never from evaluated-arm scores or field values.
gates.required_evidence: Schema validation plus injected score/rank mutations that leave gold labels unchanged.

gates.requirement: At least one same-evidence state has a unique relevance argmax that is unsafe, constraint-invalid, or cost-suboptimal; the oracle returns a unique gold action, exact regret, and explicit unsafe refusal.
gates.required_evidence: Deterministic replay, relevance-mutation, unsafe-refusal, no-feasible, tie, and commitment-tamper tests.

gates.requirement: The compiler emits KEEP, SUPERSEDE, CONTRADICT, and COMPENSATE events with stable identity/digest bindings, valid_time, observed_at, and repeated per-fact revision sequence.
Every registered query time has deterministic current and as-of cuts with stale/current status, contradiction history, and compensation-preserved history.
gates.required_evidence: Delayed-observation, stale/current/as-of, contradiction, resolution, and compensation tests.

gates.requirement: All registered branch permutations are generated; declared commutative branches have one valid semantic outcome, while non-confluent branches have divergent outcomes and a non-empty registered reason.
gates.required_evidence: Positive commutative permutation test plus misdeclared confluence and missing/false reason refusals.

gates.requirement: Duplicate IDs with differing payloads, missing evidence/time, bad operations, invalid or cyclic supersession, ambiguous current cuts, noncanonical JSON, unknown evidence, oracle ties, and commitment tamper are rejected.
gates.required_evidence: Injected negative tests covering every listed refusal class.

gates.requirement: Roles A, B, C, and D implement the frozen interfaces above; A and B pass executable hidden-head and exact-B inventory checks, while C and D remain controls.
gates.required_evidence: Executable arm entrypoints, parameter inventory, serialized-state inventory, and hidden-head attack receipt.

gates.requirement: Development and confirmatory sources are disjoint, byte-hash-bound, contamination-audited, and public labels are not called sealed.
gates.required_evidence: Frozen source/split manifests and contamination audit created before G4 measurement admission.

gates.requirement: G2 artifacts implement tasks and interfaces only; no winner, efficacy, production, shared-scalar, or public benchmark result is asserted.
gates.required_evidence: Claim-boundary validation and absence of result payloads.

claim_boundary.statement: These task fixtures and interface expectations do not implement evaluated model arms and are not evidence for or against the shared-field hypothesis.
